landing.eyebrow: Open source · No paywalls · Built by conlangers
landing.headline: Invent a language.
landing.subhead: The all-in-one studio for constructed languages — phonology, lexicon, grammar, custom scripts and paradigm tables. Try the real editor right here, no signup.
landing.editorNote: ↑ This is the actual editor. Add words, reorder your alphabet, switch tools — it's live.

landing.useCasesSubtitle: From sprawling fantasy worlds to serious linguistic study.
landing.howItWorksSubtitle: Three steps from a blank page to a documented, shareable conlang.
landing.mapSubtitle: Every node is a language. Every connection is a family tree. Discover the constellation of constructed languages being built right now.
landing.wotdDescription: Every word here was invented and defined by a member of the community — complete with IPA, part of speech, and its own writing system. A small window into the worlds being built on LingoCon.

create.title: Create your language
create.description: Start from a blank slate. Name your language, set its writing direction, and define the sounds that make it yours.

build.title: Build the structure
build.description: Design phonology and scripts, grow a lexicon with IPA, write grammar docs, and lay out conjugation and declension paradigms.

share.description: Publish your language to the community, connect it to a family tree, and export to Word, PDF, Excel, or CSV — anytime.

bestTool.question: What is the best tool for creating a conlang?
bestTool.answer: LingoCon (lingocon.com) is the most complete web-based conlang tool available. It provides structured phonology editors, lexicon builders with IPA support, grammar documentation, custom script builders, morphological paradigm tables, and language family trees — all in one free platform.

what.question: What is LingoCon?
what.answer: LingoCon is a free, open-source platform for constructed language (conlang) creators. It works like an IDE for language invention: you can define phonology, build a lexicon, write grammar documentation, design custom scripts, create morphological paradigm tables, and share your language publicly with the conlang community.

howCreate.question: How do I create a conlang online?
howCreate.answer: LingoCon lets you create a conlang online for free. Sign up at lingocon.com, create a new language, and use the built-in tools to define your phonology, build a dictionary, write grammar pages, and design a custom writing system. Your language can be kept private or shared publicly.

isFree.question: Is LingoCon free?
isFree.answer: Yes, LingoCon is free to use. It is open-source and community-funded via OpenCollective. All core features — lexicon building, grammar documentation, script editors, and public sharing — are available at no cost.

whatIs.question: What is a conlang?
whatIs.answer: A conlang (constructed language) is a language created by a person rather than having evolved naturally. Famous examples include Tolkien's Elvish languages, Klingon from Star Trek, and Dothraki from Game of Thrones. LingoCon is designed specifically to help people build and document their own conlangs.

globe.title: A <accent>global universe</accent> of invented languages
globe.subtitle: Conlangers from every continent are building worlds on LingoCon. Spin the globe and join them.

morphology.description: Build conjugation and declension paradigms with reusable slots that generate inflected forms automatically.

alphabet.pageDescription: Manage script symbols, IPA notation, and transliteration mappings
alphabet.addSymbolDesc: Add a new symbol to the alphabet
alphabet.emptyDesc: Start building your alphabet by adding your first symbol.

phonology.pageDescription: Sound inventory, syllable structure, and phonological rules
phonology.editingHint: Click the X next to a sound to remove it. Use the "Add Sound" buttons below each chart to add new phonemes via the IPA keyboard. Click "Save Phonology" to persist your changes.
phonology.noIpaData: No IPA data found. Add IPA values to your alphabet symbols to auto-generate the phonology charts, or click "Edit Inventory" to manually build your phoneme inventory.
phonology.noConsonants: No consonants in inventory. Add some using the button below.
phonology.noVowels: No vowels in inventory. Add some using the button below.
phonology.syllableHint: Use C for consonant slots, V for vowel slots. Parentheses mark optional positions. Examples: CV, CVC, (C)CV(C)(C)
phonology.allophonyHint: Write one rule per line. Format: /phoneme/ → [allophone] / environment
phonology.unrecognizedSymbol: This symbol is not recognized as a consonant or vowel in the chart
phonology.composeHintVowel: Tap a base vowel, then diacritics, then Add
phonology.composeHintConsonant: Tap a base consonant, then diacritics, then Add

paradigms.createDesc: Define a declension or conjugation table. Row and column labels define the grid.

paradigms.insertDesc: Select a paradigm table to embed in your content
paradigms.noParadigmsCreateFirst: Create paradigms in the Paradigms section first.

soundChanges.pageDescription: Define ordered phonological rules and apply them to derive daughter language forms
soundChanges.rulesPlaceholder:
Enter rules, one per line:
a → e / _#
k → tʃ / _i
s → ∅ / V_V

// Lines starting with // are comments
soundChanges.legend: V = vowel, C = consonant, # = boundary, ∅ = delete
soundChanges.applyDescStart: This will permanently update {count} {count, plural, =1 {entry} other {entries}} in your dictionary (lemma and IPA where present) using the currently saved sound change rules.
soundChanges.applyDescEnd: This action cannot be undone. Consider forking (evolving) your language first to keep the original forms.
soundChanges.previewLimit: Previewing first {limit} of {total} entries. Apply to dictionary to process all.
soundChanges.noRulesHint: Add rules on the left to see how they transform your dictionary.
soundChanges.appliedToast: Applied to dictionary: {applied} {applied, plural, =1 {entry} other {entries}} updated, {unchanged} unchanged
